actionable_feedback: Provide a parseable workbook or explicit per-cell citations: ensure PBPK_QC_3.xlsx is saved in a standard .xlsx format readable by openpyxl, and/or include a CSV export of calculation and sensitivity sheets. If you cannot change the workbook file format, add a table in the memo listing each transcribed value with exact workbook coordinates (e.g., SheetName!A12) and any adjacent cell comments.

Make formulas visible in the workbook (no pasted final values in cells that should contain formulas). Annotate key formula cells with comments that reference the memo (e.g., 'see memo: Calculation Results, O3 multiplier applied at SheetX!C5').

Clarify trigger detection language in the memo. Explicitly state for each record (R1,R2,R3) whether each trigger fired, why, and which trigger(s) were not evaluated due to missing comparator. Reconcile contradictory statements (for example, do not claim O3 'directly addresses the only rule-flagged record' while also stating it does not clear logP>5).

Add a dedicated sensitivity worksheet (or CSV) that shows the sensitivity-run design: comparator value (or 'missing'), ranges/step-sizes tested, metrics tracked, and summarized impacts on outputs. If comparator is missing, explicitly mark comparator-based triggers as 'not run' and list recommended comparator acquisition steps.

Quantify post-remediation values for the recommended option(s) and state whether remediation fully clears the flagged rule(s) or only partially mitigates them. Include arithmetic examples for the flagged record(s) (e.g., R2 logP before and after O3, and whether post-remediation logP<=5 if that is the acceptance criterion).
Ensure cost-summation cells are visible and formulas are present; annotate these cells to show how total_recommended_cost_usd was computed and confirm budget compliance.

After implementing the above, re-run verification and note in the memo where each verifier concern was addressed (e.g., 'See Sheet Sensitivity! and memo table mapping cell coordinates for ri_3 resolution').
artifact_specific_comments: expert_memo.md: Good structure (calculation results, assumptions, missing inputs, expert-review questions) but contains an internal inconsistency regarding trigger identification and remediation clearing; update to be unambiguous.

feedback_summary: Baseline deliverables are largely present and well documented (assumptions, costs, multiplicative remediation arithmetic). Two issues prevented full credit for sensitivity analysis & trigger compliance (ri_3): (1) the workbook could not be programmatically parsed in the preview, preventing independent verification of cell-level formulas, comments, and dedicated sensitivity worksheets; (2) the memo contains an internal inconsistency about which trigger(s) were flagged and whether the recommended remediation (O3) fully clears the flagged condition. Addressing these will raise confidence and remove the ri_3 penalty.
